[[Kuflink Stadium]] | avg_attendance = 1461 | high_attendance = 6752 | top_tries = [[Paul Ulberg]] - 16 | top_goals = [[Oli Leyland]] - 52 | top_points = Oli Leyland - 112 | pattern_la1 = _yellowborder | pattern_b1 = _Vwideyellow | pattern_ra1 = _yellowborder | pattern_sh1 = _yellowsides | pattern_so1 = 0000FF | leftarm1 = 0000FF | body1 = 0000FF | rightarm1 = 0000FF | shorts1 = 0000FF | socks1 = 0000FF | pattern_la2 = _redborder | pattern_b2 = _vwhiteblackred | pattern_ra2 = _redborder | pattern_sh2 = | pattern_so2 = _redtop | leftarm2 = 000000 | body2 = 000000 | rightarm2 = 000000 | shorts2 = 000000 | socks2 = 000000 | prev_season = [[2021 London Broncos season|2021]] | next_season = [[2023 London Broncos season|2023]] | list = yes | list_link = List of London Broncos seasons }} The '''2022 London Broncos season''' is the 43rd year in the club's history, the third consecutive season out of the [[Super League]] and their first season at [[Plough Lane]] as tenants of [[AFC Wimbledon]]. They were coached by [[Jermaine Coleman (rugby league)|Jermaine Coleman]] between January and May 2022, and [[Mike Eccles]] from May 2022 onwards. The Broncos competed in both the [[2022 RFL Championship|2022 Betfred Championship]] and the [[2022 Challenge Cup]]. Head coach [[Jermaine Coleman (rugby league)|Jermaine Coleman]] assistant coach [[Jy-mel Coleman]] were relieved of their duties by mutual consent in May 2022.[https://londonbroncosrl.com/2022/05/24/club-statement-jermaine-and-jy-mel-coleman/ Club statement – Jermaine and Jy-mel Coleman] [[File:Plough Lane.jpg|thumb|right|upright=1.2|Plough Lane as the
